title: Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME00121, a complete encoding of Karl Breul's 1890 article 'Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols' on printed pp.401–408. Breul directly transcribed two independent medieval carols in 1882 from the manuscript now National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.3.1: the Nativity/Epiphany carol at current ff.59r–59v and the Annunciation carol at ff.89v–90v. The complete 482-canvas NLS digital object, complete 496-page Englische Studien volume, and complete 1843 Turnbull volume are cached with fixity and exact work portions. Turnbull independently printed both NLS texts in 1843. DIMEV v1.0 records one collateral witness for the Annunciation carol and three for the Nativity carol; current official catalog and request routes are recorded, but no complete public facsimile was found for those four collateral manuscripts.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact at CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00121.xml; the U-M encoding, Breul and Turnbull editions, manuscript witnesses, facsimiles, and DIMEV descriptions are separate entities.
CME00121 encodes the complete Breul article, not the whole Englische Studien volume. The article contains two independent medieval works and two substantial German commentary sections.

The Annunciation poem has 56 medieval lines in seven eight-line stanzas. Two further L elements embedded in the second LG are Breul's Chaucer epigraph and are excluded from the historical poem metric.

Breul says the Nativity/Epiphany carol begins at fol.59vo ff.; DIMEV and direct current NLS image inspection instead establish f.59r–59v. Both attributed statements are preserved, and current foliation controls present-day access locators.
Breul's printed italics mark every departure from manuscript readings, including editorial supplies and normalization; XML HI/@REND='italic' is not evidence of manuscript emphasis.
DIMEV's Turnbull page range '13-40' for record 589 is a data error. Direct inspection of the complete 1843 scan confirms pp.139–140; the Annunciation carol follows on pp.141–143.
The NLS cache is complete relative to all 482 provider-published canvases, not every physical leaf: the provider says numerous modern blank opening flyleaves were not all captured.

The pinned CME root LICENSE uses Unlicense language framed as software. Its application to corpus XML/data/markup remains unresolved and is not broadened into a high-confidence repository-artifact rights claim.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00121
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00121; the file is 27,935 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00121
entities.description: The complete keyed electronic text of Breul's eight-page article, including both carols, German commentary, notes, comparative quotations, final source statement, dateline, and signature.

entities.label: Karl Breul, 'Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols' (1890)
entities.description: The immediate print source and complete article encoded by CME00121. Breul prints two direct 1882 transcriptions from Advocates' Library MS 19.3.1 with textual notes and extensive German linguistic, literary, biblical, and comparative commentary.

bibliographic.citation: Karl Breul, 'Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols,' Englische Studien: Organ für englische Philologie 14 (Leipzig: O. R. Reisland, 1890), 401–408.
bibliographic.title: Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols
bibliographic.container_title: Englische Studien: Organ für englische Philologie

notes: Breul says he transcribed both poems in 1882 together with Sir Gowther and the Boke of Curtesy.
He dates both probably to the fifteenth century but says their original dialects cannot be determined from rhyme.
He retains variable manuscript spelling while normalizing capitalization of proper names and line starts, distinguishing u/v, and joining compounds; every textual departure is italicized.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Englische Studien volume 14 (1890)
entities.description: A complete 496-page scan of the journal volume. Breul's article occupies printed pp.401–408, PDF pp.411–418, and Internet Archive leaf views n410–n417.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume 496-page PDF with OCR and scan metadata derivatives
notes: Internet Archive metadata incorrectly gives 1877 and description '26'; the visually inspected volume title page and article establish volume 14, Leipzig, 1890.

entities.label: William Barclay David Donald Turnbull, The Visions of Tundale (1843)
entities.description: An earlier independent edition from the same Advocates' Library manuscript, then designated Jac. V.7.27. Turnbull prints the Nativity/Epiphany carol on pp.139–140 and the Annunciation carol on pp.141–143.

bibliographic.citation: William Barclay David Donald Turnbull, ed., The Visions of Tundale; together with Metrical Moralizations and Other Fragments of Early Poetry, Hitherto Inedited (Edinburgh: Thomas G. Stevenson, 1843), 139–143.
bibliographic.title: The Visions of Tundale; together with Metrical Moralizations and Other Fragments of Early Poetry, Hitherto Inedited

notes: Turnbull describes the source as the Advocates' Library manuscript Jac. V.7.27, a small fifteenth-century quarto of 216 folios; this is the current NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1.
No dependence of Breul's 1890 transcription on Turnbull is asserted: Breul explicitly says he copied both poems from the manuscript in 1882.

entities.label: Google/Internet Archive complete scan of Turnbull's The Visions of Tundale (1843)
entities.description: A complete 191-page Google scan from the Harvard copy, distributed by Internet Archive as PDF with OCR and scan metadata. The two carols occupy PDF pp.160–164.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume 191-page PDF with OCR and scan metadata derivatives

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: The direct source of both Breul transcriptions and Turnbull's earlier printings. The Nativity/Epiphany carol occupies current ff.59r–59v; the Annunciation carol occupies ff.89v–90v.

holding.notes: Acquired for the national collections in 1925.

physical_description.extent: xii + 216 + xviii folios; target carols at current ff.59r–59v and 89v–90v
notes: The codex was written in the North Midlands mostly by Richard Heeg, with material by James Hawghton and additions in other hands; no target-carol hand is assigned here without passage-specific evidence.
Breul's historical fol.59vo ff. locus for the Nativity carol conflicts with current direct-image and DIMEV f.59r–59v evidence.

entities.label: NLS digital facsimile of Adv.MS.19.3.1
entities.description: The complete 482-canvas object exposed by the NLS IIIF manifest, including provider-published covers and endmatter. The Nativity/Epiphany carol is at canvases 133–134 and the Annunciation carol at canvases 194–196. NLS notes that numerous modern blank opening flyleaves were not all captured.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 482 color canvases

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Add. 5943
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century English composite manuscript containing a four-stanza version of Ecce ancilla domini at f.182v. DIMEV says its first stanza has seven lines and the remaining three have eight; this is a collateral version, not Breul's direct source.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.value: early fifteenth century; approximately 1415 in DIAMM/RISM description

physical_description.extent: iii parchment + 177 paper + iii parchment folios; Annunciation carol at f.182v under the manuscript's historical foliation
physical_description.format: Composite manuscript containing astronomical, moral, sermonic, administrative, and musical material
notes: DIAMM associates the codex with England and Hinton Priory in Somerset; this provenance is not converted into a work-composition claim.
The current public Cambridge finding-aid routes checked did not expose a complete facsimile. DIAMM lists images behind login, but their coverage and target-folio availability were not established.

entities.label: Phillipa Hardman, The Heege Manuscript (2000)
entities.description: A complete scholarly print facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1, published as Leeds Texts and Monographs, New Series 16. DIMEV cites it for both NLS carol witnesses; it remains useful as a fixed scholarly reproduction even though NLS now publishes a complete 482-canvas digital object.

bibliographic.citation: Phillipa Hardman, The Heege Manuscript: A Facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1, Leeds Texts and Monographs, New Series 16 (Leeds: University of Leeds, 2000).
bibliographic.title: The Heege Manuscript: A Facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1
bibliographic.series: Leeds Texts and Monographs, New Series 16

notes: The GBV URL https://www.gbv.de/dms/goettingen/349484066.pdf is only a two-page title-and-contents preview, not the manuscript facsimile; it is cached solely as metadata evidence and never represented as complete facsimile access.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Bodl. 77
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English paper composite in three parts. DIMEV identifies fragmentary text of As Holy Kirk Makes Mind on binding strips at ff.1v and 2; the official summary catalog does not enumerate that fragment.

physical_description.extent: Composite in three parts; target survives fragmentarily on binding strips at ff.1v and 2 according to DIMEV
notes: All three cataloged parts are fifteenth-century and English.
The fragment's binding-strip status comes from DIMEV v1.0 and must not be presented as if it were stated in the abbreviated Bodleian catalog.

entities.label: British Library, Egerton MS 3307
entities.description: The Windsor Carol-book, probably written for St George's Chapel, Windsor, in 1430–1444. At ff.65v–66 it preserves a five-stanza musical version of As Holy Kirk Makes Mind with the burden 'Almyghty ihesu kynge of blysse' and a variant four-line burden structure.

physical_description.extent: ii + 88 folios; target version at ff.65v–66
notes: The current exact catalog record has an empty Digitised Content field.
The official record requires a letter of introduction for consultation and says all manuscripts and archives must be consulted at the Library in London.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 275
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English paper manuscript containing a variant of As Holy Kirk Makes Mind at ff.146v–147, with the burden 'Joy we all now yn this feste / For verbum caro factum est'. DIMEV also reports an approximately 1818 transcript in BL Add MS 39864, f.160.

physical_description.extent: ff.1*–2* + 160 + flyleaves; target version at ff.146v–147
physical_description.format: Bound religious and literary miscellany in multiple hands
notes: The current exact catalog record has an empty Digitised Content field.
The circa-1818 Add MS 39864 transcript is recorded as a supporting reproduction lead, not treated as a medieval witness or a demonstrated source of Harley 275.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records 1173 and 589
entities.description: Versioned scholarly index records for Ecce ancilla domini (IMEV/NIMEV 707) and As Holy Kirk Makes Mind / Intravit ventris thalamum (IMEV/NIMEV 340), documenting forms, subjects, incipits, known witnesses, loci, variants, facsimiles, and editions.
notes: Record 1173 describes two witnesses: CUL Add. 5943 f.182v and NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1 ff.89v–90v.
Record 589 describes four witnesses: Bodley 77 ff.1v and 2, Egerton 3307 ff.65v–66, Harley 275 ff.146v–147, and NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1 f.59r–59v.
Record 589's Turnbull page value '13-40' conflicts with direct scan evidence for pp.139–140 and is preserved as an attributed data error.

access.access_method: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest and IIIF Image API

work_portion.label: Two noncontiguous CME00121 carol portions in Adv.MS.19.3.1
locators: Nativity/Epiphany: current ff.59r–59v = IIIF canvases 133–134 = ZIP images/000133.jpg–images/000134.jpg
Annunciation: current ff.89v–90v = IIIF canvases 194–196 = ZIP images/000194.jpg–images/000196.jpg
notes: The start/end URLs delimit a discontiguous envelope. The exact Nativity endpoint is canvas 134 at https://dg-view.nls.uk/iiif/2/1341%2F6318%2F134163183.5/full/1600,/0/default.jpg; the exact Annunciation start is canvas 194 at https://dg-view.nls.uk/iiif/2/1341%2F6390%2F134163903.5/full/1600,/0/default.jpg.
Canvas 132 is a preceding-page control and canvas 135 is a following-page control for the Nativity carol; canvas 193 precedes and canvas 197 follows the Annunciation carol.
All 482 canvases exposed by the provider manifest were captured at a non-upscaling 1600-pixel-wide request. The provider says numerous modern blank flyleaves at the beginning were not all captured.

access.format: IIIF images and manifest

qpdf succeeds with nonfatal linearization-hint warnings; the file has exactly two pages.

access.format: Complete printed scholarly facsimile; University of Leeds listed price £82.50
notes: Price and stock can change; verify the Leeds product page before ordering.
The cached preview is metadata-only. Access to the complete facsimile is purchase or library consultation, not a public download.

access.access_method: Register for Cambridge University Library Special Collections, request MS Add. 5943 in advance, and confirm availability before travel
access.format: Physical manuscript

access.access_method: Request research scans or publication-quality imaging through Cambridge Special Collections imaging services; ask first whether a complete existing surrogate can be supplied
access.format: Research PDF or separately ordered publication-quality images
notes: A whole-object request should explicitly name both boards, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, every surviving leaf, and color/scale targets where offered; identify f.182v as the work portion without requesting only that leaf.

access.access_method: Log in through the DIAMM source record to test the advertised image viewer and authenticated manifest
access.format: Login-gated DIAMM manuscript page images and JSON manifest
notes: The cached source page advertises ‘Images (Log in to view)’, while an unauthenticated request to the exact manifest URL returned HTTP 401 with JSON rather than image metadata.

The image-set coverage and whether f.182v is present remain unknown because authenticated access was not tested. This restricted route must not be counted as a complete facsimile or as target-work coverage until its inventory is inspected.

access.access_method: Checked Cambridge Digital Library, Cambridge finding-aid routes, and DIAMM source 328
access.format: No complete public manuscript facsimile verified
notes: This finding concerns only the absence of a verified complete, openly accessible facsimile. DIAMM's separate login-gated image route is recorded under access:cul-add5943-diamm-images; its coverage and target-folio presence remain unverified.

rights.component: Modern repository XML structure, data, and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense text but frames its dedication and permission as applying to 'software'. No path-specific notice was found for source/CME_phase_3/CME00121.xml, and the root wording does not expressly resolve whether corpus XML, encoded data, or markup is within that software dedication.
notes: The exact root license is retained as evidence, not overextended into a high-confidence rights conclusion. Underlying medieval texts, historical editions, U-M content, catalog data, and provider images have separate records.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and represented public-domain source material
rights.basis: The U-M CME collection applies No Copyright - United States to public content; that rights statement records no known U.S. copyright rather than granting a reuse license. Institutional service terms still apply.

rights.component: Breul's 1890 scholarly edition and commentary
rights.basis: The article was first published in 1890, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Internet Archive complete-volume PDF, OCR, and scan derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1890 journal volume is public domain in the United States, but the item metadata supplies no item-specific open license or rights statement for the exact scan files.
notes: Public download and research caching are recorded separately from permission to redistribute the provider's exact scan.

rights.component: Turnbull's 1843 scholarly edition
rights.basis: The volume was first published in 1843, before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Google-derived page images, Internet Archive PDF, OCR, and scan derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1843 edition is public domain, but the PDF embeds Google usage language requesting personal noncommercial use, attribution, and no automated querying, and the item supplies no open license for the exact scan files.
notes: This record does not decide the enforceability or copyright effect of the embedded boilerplate; it preserves the provider's stated conditions separately from the public-domain underlying edition.

rights.component: Underlying medieval carol texts embodied in Adv.MS.19.3.1; physical ownership and digital images excluded
rights.basis: The anonymous texts are embodied in a manuscript copied about 1480 and predate modern copyright by centuries. Physical-access and image-reuse terms are separate.

rights.component: NLS digital page images and item-level facsimile
rights.basis: The exact IIIF item metadata links the Public Domain Mark 1.0 and states that the material is free of known copyright restrictions. NLS guidance says faithful digitizations retain the original's status and waives related digitization rights with CC0 where they subsist; the exact item remains marked PDM rather than licensed.

rights.component: Hardman's modern 2000 facsimile publication, editorial matter, selection and presentation, including the two-page preview
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and applicable publication jurisdictions
rights.basis: The facsimile was published in 2000 and no open license or public-domain dedication was found. Purchase or library access does not grant redistribution rights in the modern book or its scanned preview.
notes: The underlying medieval manuscript texts remain public domain; this claim concerns the modern facsimile publication and exact preview file.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Annunciation text embodied in CUL Add. 5943; physical ownership, catalog data, and images excluded
rights.basis: The anonymous text is embodied in an early-fifteenth-century manuscript and predates modern copyright by centuries.

rights.component: Any newly supplied Cambridge University Library manuscript images
rights.basis: Cambridge distinguishes research scans from publication-quality imaging and conditions supply and reuse on service and item-specific terms; no item-level open license was found for Add. 5943.

rights.component: Modern DIAMM source-record text, structure, and cached HTML for CUL Add. 5943; medieval text and manuscript images excluded
rights.basis: The DIAMM page expressly labels reproduced catalog content as copyright and warns against copying or reproduction without permission. Public web access and research caching do not create an open-data license.
notes: This record concerns only the cached modern catalog page; the anonymous medieval Annunciation text has a separate public-domain record.

rights.component: Existing manuscript page images advertised by DIAMM for CUL Add. 5943; medieval text, catalog data, and any newly commissioned CUL imaging excluded
rights.basis: The DIAMM source page marks its existing images © 2002 Cambridge University Library and supplies no open reuse license; the exact manifest also requires authentication.
notes: Registration-based viewing, if granted, is access only and does not establish permission to redistribute the image files.

rights.component: Underlying medieval carol fragments embodied in MS. Bodl. 77; physical ownership, catalog data, and images excluded
rights.basis: The anonymous text is embodied in a fifteenth-century manuscript and predates modern copyright by centuries.

rights.component: Any newly ordered Bodleian digital images
rights.basis: Bodleian Imaging Services supplies new files for a fee, and reuse remains governed by service terms and item-specific permissions rather than inferred from the medieval text's status.

rights.component: Modern Bodleian catalog description, TEI/XML structure, and cached catalog file; medieval text and manuscript images excluded
rights.basis: The catalog record is publicly downloadable, but no item-level open license for the exact descriptive text or TEI/XML was established in this review. Public access is not treated as permission for unrestricted republication.

rights.component: Underlying medieval carol version embodied in Egerton MS 3307; physical ownership and images excluded
rights.basis: The anonymous text is embodied in a 1430–1444 manuscript and predates modern copyright by centuries.

editorial_practices.description: CME00121 begins with printed p.401 and ends with Breul's signature on p.408. It does not encode adjacent journal articles, but no part of Breul's article is omitted.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two noncontiguous carol portions in Adv.MS.19.3.1
editorial_practices.representation: Complete transcriptions at the beginning of a signed scholarly article
editorial_practices.description: Breul selects the Nativity/Epiphany carol at current ff.59r–59v and the Annunciation carol at ff.89v–90v from a much larger manuscript; neither complete-codex context nor neighboring works are reproduced in the article.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Variable manuscript capitalization of proper names and at line beginnings
editorial_practices.representation: Proper names and line openings consistently capitalized
editorial_practices.description: Breul explicitly lists capitalization of David, Mary, and Gabriell and initial capitals at every line among his normalizations. He separately distinguishes u from v and joins compounds while otherwise retaining spelling variation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Readings and graphic forms in Adv.MS.19.3.1
editorial_practices.representation: Every departure in Breul's text printed in italics and retained by CME00121 as HI/@REND='italic'
editorial_practices.description: Breul's final statement defines italics as a record of deviations from manuscript readings. The XML contains 129 italic HI elements, 96 in the poem divisions and 33 in commentary. This typographic device records editorial intervention, not manuscript emphasis, and each case requires manuscript comparison to distinguish expansion, correction, and normalization.
editorial_practices.scope: Complete article, especially the two carol texts

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, source/CME_phase_3/CME00121.xml at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Direct repository evidence for the complete encoded article, IDs, structural boundaries, poem and commentary text, editorial notes, page references, source statement, dateline, signature, and modern revision description.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: complete 1,211-byte root license; git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense public-domain dedication and permission language but repeatedly describes its subject as software. No separate path-level license notice was found for CME00121.xml, leaving application to corpus XML/data/markup unresolved.

evidence.citation: XPath and direct structural audit of CME00121.xml at the pinned CME commit, performed 2026-07-13.
evidence.locator: DIV1, DIV2, P, Q, LG, L, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, HI, CLOSER, DATELINE, and SIGNED elements
evidence.summary: Computed one DIV1, four DIV2, 32 P, four Q, 22 LG, 136 raw L, 12 NOTE, nine PB, 18 MILESTONE, and 129 italic HI. The poem divisions contain 96 raw L, but two are Breul's Chaucer epigraph, leaving 94 medieval carol lines; commentary contains 40 quoted L. No numbered empty heading-only units were found.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00121 item routes.
evidence.summary: Official item identity and stable URL for CME00121. The keyed repository artifact independently preserves the complete article and U-M identifiers; automated requests to the stable route returned an access-denial response during this review.
notes: Automated denial is not treated as proof that ordinary browser access or the official item has been withdrawn.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection information and copyright statement.
evidence.summary: Collection-level evidence for manually keyed public-domain editions, in-house proofing and tagging, XML context, current access framing, and the No Copyright - United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Karl Breul, 'Zwei mittelenglische Christmas Carols,' Englische Studien 14 (1890): 401–408, audited in the complete volume scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pp.411–418 = printed pp.401–408; volume title page also inspected
evidence.summary: Confirms the article and complete-volume titles, editor signature, 1890 Leipzig imprint, article boundaries, both poem texts, commentary, the two-line Chaucer epigraph, biblical citations, source manuscript and historical loci, 1882 transcription statement, probable fifteenth-century dating, dialect cautions, normalization practices, and italic deviation marking. qpdf passes all 496 pages.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for englischestudie14leipuoft.
evidence.locator: metadata object and derivative file inventory
evidence.summary: Supports the stable item identifier, Robarts/University of Toronto source and MSN sponsorship, file inventory, and absence of an item-specific license. Its erroneous 1877 date and description '26' are rejected in favor of the inspected title page and article.

evidence.citation: William Barclay David Donald Turnbull, ed., The Visions of Tundale (Edinburgh, 1843), complete scan audited at pp.139–143.
evidence.locator: PDF pp.160–164 = printed pp.139–143; title page and introduction also inspected
evidence.summary: Confirms the 1843 title and imprint, Turnbull's direct manuscript designation Jac. V.7.27 and physical description, Nativity/Epiphany text at pp.139–140, Annunciation text at pp.141–143, and the exact correction to DIMEV's dropped-9 page value. qpdf passes all 191 pages. The embedded Google boilerplate requests personal noncommercial use and attribution.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for visionstundalet00turngoog.
evidence.locator: metadata object and derivative file inventory
evidence.summary: Confirms the stable item identifier, 1843 date, Turnbull editor attribution, Harvard source copy, Google sponsorship and scanning, Internet Archive distribution, contents list including both target carols, and absence of an item-specific open license.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Adv.MS.19.3.1 catalog record.
evidence.locator: Contents, Physical Description, Arrangement, access, and reproduction context
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the c.1480 paper codex, North-Midland and scribal context, current and former shelfmarks, xii + 216 + xviii extent, 210 × 140 mm dimensions, acquisition, and access route.
notes: Automated requests returned HTTP 403 during this review; exact catalog facts are corroborated by the live IIIF manifest and prior official-record capture.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, IIIF manifest for Adv.MS.19.3.1.
evidence.summary: Exact provider evidence for shelfmark, c.1480 date, North-Midland and scribal description, 482-canvas published object, omission of some modern blank opening flyleaves, and item-level Public Domain Mark statement. The cached ZIP contains the canonical manifest, inventory, and all 482 exposed images.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of NLS Adv.MS.19.3.1 target and control canvases for both CME00121 carols.
evidence.locator: Nativity/Epiphany canvases 133–134 with controls 132 and 135; Annunciation canvases 194–196 with controls 193 and 197
evidence.summary: Confirmed the Nativity/Epiphany carol begins on current f.59r at canvas 133 and ends on f.59v at canvas 134, contradicting Breul's historical fol.59vo ff. start. Confirmed the Annunciation carol begins on f.89v at canvas 194 and ends on f.90v at canvas 196. The exact boundary Image API URLs returned HTTP 200.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Copyright and the collections.
evidence.summary: Official policy evidence that faithful digitizations retain the original's copyright status and that NLS waives related digitization rights with CC0 where they subsist; the exact IIIF object itself carries the Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: University of Leeds, LTM16: Phillipa Hardman, The Heege Manuscript: A Facsimile of National Library of Scotland MS Advocates 19.3.1.
evidence.summary: Official current purchase evidence for the complete 2000 printed facsimile, title, author, series LTM New Series 16, ISBN 9780902296268, £82.50 listed price, and bibliographic/library access via OCLC 51223769.
notes: Price and stock are time-sensitive and should be rechecked before ordering. No lawful complete public digital copy was found.

evidence.citation: GBV, title-and-contents preview for Phillipa Hardman, The Heege Manuscript (2000).
evidence.locator: two pages only: title page and contents
evidence.summary: The 27,796-byte PDF is lawful bibliographic preview evidence but contains no manuscript facsimile leaves. It is cached metadata-only and must not be treated as digitization of Adv.MS.19.3.1 or the complete Hardman volume.
notes: qpdf reports two pages and succeeds with nonfatal linearization-hint warnings.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0 source-data release, records 1173 and 589.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml records 1173 and 589; associated Manuscripts.xml and Bibliography.xml entries; README and LICENSE
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for DIMEV/IMEV/NIMEV identities, titles, subjects, forms, incipits, two Annunciation witnesses, four Nativity witnesses, exact loci, variant extents and burdens, Turnbull and Breul editions, Hardman 2000 facsimile citations, and CC BY 4.0 licensing. The complete ZIP passes integrity at release commit 5ad5f592868471b44fc72f6e2f42db419780b42c.

evidence.citation: Digital Image Archive of Medieval Music, source 328, GB-Cu Add. 5943, supplemented by DIMEV v1.0 witness wit-1173-1.
evidence.locator: source description, provenance, material, dimensions, and image-access notice; DIMEV record 1173 for f.182v and four-stanza extent
evidence.summary: Supports CUL Add. 5943 as an early-fifteenth-century English mixed-support composite, its Hinton Priory provenance, current holding, and the four-stanza Ecce ancilla domini witness at f.182v. The DIAMM page labels reproduced catalog content as copyright and offers images only after login.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Visiting Special Collections and Scan & Deliver for Special Collections.
evidence.summary: Official routes for reader registration, advance manuscript requests, Special Collections contact, research scans, and publication-quality imaging. No complete CUDL object for Add. 5943 was verified in the routes checked.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts catalog, MS. Bodl. 77 (manuscript_1755; Summary Catalogue 2265).
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, three-part composite structure, paper support, English origin, fifteenth-century dating of all parts, Summary Catalogue number, holding, and enquiry address. The abbreviated catalog does not enumerate the binding-strip carol; that content claim comes from DIMEV.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Official route for requesting and purchasing new manuscript photography, subject to collection condition, assessment, fees, service terms, and item-specific reuse permissions.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 3307 and Harley MS 275.
evidence.locator: Egerton item (u) ff.65v–66 and Harley Christmas carol at ff.146v–147; physical descriptions, dates, access notes, and empty Digitised Content fields
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the Windsor Carol-book's 1430–1444 date, vellum extent, dimensions, musical carol and access conditions, and for Harley 275's fifteenth-century English paper miscellany, exact Christmas-carol locus, extent, dimensions, multiple hands, and absent digitized-content link. Exact JSON files are cached with per-access fixity.
notes: Cached Egerton JSON: source-cache/CME00121/bl-egerton-ms-3307-catalog.json, SHA-256 3a0ec495fd8547c8c1b0c403a1f9c9c4c040d00fded7d22635def88dae81a2fb, 23,590 bytes.
Cached Harley JSON: source-cache/CME00121/bl-harley-ms-275-catalog.json, SHA-256 6e9a061cc7af41c2eea1157c38629f5b3a31307e22fe2e6927f68c2fcdc9a598, 25,858 bytes.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts item request and Reference Services routes, checked for Egerton MS 3307 and Harley MS 275.
evidence.summary: Official current routes for requesting physical manuscripts and making shelfmark-specific enquiries. Both exact catalog records have empty Digitised Content fields; no complete public facsimile was verified.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, catalog-text license footer on the Egerton MS 3307 and Harley MS 275 records.
evidence.locator: page footer: British Library Board copyright and CC-BY license statement
evidence.summary: The live official record pages state that all catalog text is © British Library Board and available under CC BY except where otherwise stated. The linked license is Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International; this does not by itself establish a separate versioned license for JSON service structure or manuscript images.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.summary: Official image-order and terms routes establish fee-based digitisation, item and condition review, and the distinction between supplied image files and permission to reproduce or publish them.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, What Is Copyright?, current duration guidance.
evidence.summary: Current official guidance states that works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain because their copyright terms have expired; centuries-old anonymous manuscript texts likewise predate U.S. copyright protection.

evidence.citation: Creative Commons, Public Domain Mark 1.0.
evidence.summary: Defines the provider-linked mark as identifying material free of known copyright restrictions rather than operating as a license.

evidence.citation: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.
evidence.summary: Defines the attribution license named in the DIMEV version 1.0 release.

open_questions.question: Do complete public or restricted digital facsimiles exist for CUL Add. 5943, Bodley 77, Egerton MS 3307, or Harley MS 275 beyond the official routes checked on 2026-07-13?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge whether DIAMM's login-gated Add. 5943 images include f.182v and whether a complete existing surrogate can be supplied.
Ask Bodleian Imaging Services whether complete masters exist for Bodley 77 and whether both sides of the binding strips can be reproduced safely.
Submit separate British Library enquiries for Egerton MS 3307 and Harley MS 275 and ask whether complete masters or legacy microfilms exist before commissioning new photography.
For every supplied object, record complete-object coverage, exact target boundaries, image rights, contractual terms, and fixity before caching.

open_questions.question: Why does Breul locate the Nativity/Epiphany carol at fol.59vo ff. when current NLS images and DIMEV place the complete text on f.59r–59v?
next_steps: Ask NLS whether historical foliation, a recto/verso convention, or a simple Breul error explains the difference.
Consult Hardman 2000 and any cataloging history for foliation changes.
Continue to use current f.59r–59v and canvases 133–134 for access while preserving Breul's attributed fol.59vo ff. statement.

open_questions.question: What textual or performance genealogy connects the NLS versions with the shorter Cambridge, Bodley, Egerton, and Harley variants?
next_steps: Obtain complete images or reliable diplomatic transcriptions of all collateral witnesses.
Consult modern carol and musicological editions, including work on the Windsor Carol-book.
Do not add direct witness-to-witness edges until passage-level evidence supports them.

open_questions.question: Is a licensed complete digital copy of Hardman's 2000 Heege Manuscript facsimile available through a library platform or directly from Leeds?
next_steps: Ask the University of Leeds whether an institutional ebook or licensed digital delivery exists.
Use ISBN 9780902296268 and OCLC 51223769 to check local and interlibrary-loan holdings.
Never substitute the cached two-page GBV title-and-contents preview for the complete facsimile.

open_questions.question: Does the pinned CME repository's software-framed Unlicense declaration expressly cover corpus XML, encoded data, and markup such as CME00121.xml?
next_steps: Ask Kyvero Press to clarify the intended scope of the root Unlicense for source XML and derived data.
Add a path-level data/markup license or scope statement if the public-domain dedication is intended to cover those materials.
Until clarified, retain the exact license evidence without conflating it with U-M's No Copyright - United States statement or the medieval texts' status.
